record.field: Passbolt is a security-first, open-source password manager built for teams to store, organize and share credentials with end-to-end encryption.

Passbolt is a security-first, open-source password manager built for teams to store, organize and share credentials with end-to-end encryption.

Passbolt centralizes passwords and secrets in a self-hosted vault where every credential is encrypted client-side with OpenPGP. Each user holds their own keypair, the private key never leaves their device, and the server never sees a plaintext password, so even the administrator cannot read stored secrets. Granular permissions let teams share credentials per item, while a complete audit trail and SSO/directory integrations make it suitable for security-conscious organizations. It runs on a PHP (CakePHP) backend with a MySQL/MariaDB database and is delivered through browser extensions and native mobile and desktop apps.

Key features
  • End-to-end OpenPGP encryption with per-user keypairs and client-side encrypt/decrypt
  • Granular, per-item sharing and role-based access control for teams and groups
  • Browser extensions plus Android, iOS and Windows applications
  • Enterprise authentication: SAML SSO, Duo, YubiKey and Active Directory/LDAP sync
  • JSON API for scripting and automation, with audit logs and activity history
  • Fully self-hosted with no telemetry, optional zero-knowledge encrypted metadata and air-gapped deployment support
Best for

Security-conscious teams, IT departments, MSPs and public institutions that need to share credentials securely under their own infrastructure, with strong auditability, enterprise SSO and the ability to script and customize the deployment.

services: passbolt: image: passbolt/passbolt:latest-ce environment: APP_FULL_BASE_URL: {{ passbolt_app_full_base_url }} DATASOURCES_DEFAULT_HOST: {{ passbolt_db_host }} DATASOURCES_DEFAULT_PORT: {{ passbolt_db_port }} DATASOURCES_DEFAULT_USERNAME: {{ passbolt_db_user }} DATASOURCES_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: {{ passbolt_db_password }} DATASOURCES_DEFAULT_DATABASE: {{ passbolt_db_name }} PASSBOLT_PLUGINS_JWT_AUTHENTICATION_ENABLED: {{ passbolt_plugins_jwt_authentication_enabled }} EMAIL_DEFAULT_FROM: {{ passbolt_email_from }} EMAIL_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT_HOST: {{ passbolt_email_host }} EMAIL_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT_PORT: {{ passbolt_email_port }} EMAIL_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT_USERNAME: {{ passbolt_email_user }} EMAIL_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: {{ passbolt_email_pass }} EMAIL_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT_TLS: {{ passbolt_email_tls }} volumes: - ./gpg_volume:/etc/passbolt/gpg - ./jwt_volume:/etc/passbolt/jwt command: - /usr/bin/wait-for.sh - -t - '0' - {{ passbolt_db_host }}:{{ passbolt_db_port }} - -- - /docker-entrypoint.sh depends_on: mariadb: condition: service_healthy healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - -f - http://127.0.0.1:80 interval: 5s timeout: 20s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mariadb: image: mariadb:11 environment: MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD: {{ passbolt_db_root_password }} MARIADB_DATABASE: {{ passbolt_db_name }} MARIADB_USER: {{ passbolt_db_user }} MARIADB_PASSWORD: {{ passbolt_db_password }} volumes: - ./passbolt_mariadb_data:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD - mariadb-admin - ping - -h - localhost - --silent interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mariadb' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mariadb # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
